title: 19 Accepting Cheques on Collection
body_text:
19	Accepting Cheques on Collection
19.1	Overview of Accepting Cheques on Collection
Occasionally, a customer may want to deposit a cheque that Central 1 canât accept. For example, Central 1 can only accept foreign currency deposits in US dollars. To determine whether a cheque can be sent on deposit to Central 1, see Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, and Section 5.3, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Corporate Capture. If ineligible items are submitted via Branch Capture or Corporate Capture in error, Central 1 will issue an adjustment since the branch or the business customer retains the physical items.
If a cheque canât be deposited through Central 1 but is an otherwise acceptable item, your financial institution can request settlement directly from the drawee financial institution by sending an item on collection. The collections process can be costly. Foreign banks and their intermediaries can charge significant processing fees (sometimes more than the value of the cheque). You can also encounter lengthy settlement delays. Financial institutions arenât obligated to accept cheques on collection.
If a cheque is sent on collection and the drawee branch approves the collection request, it forwards settlement directly to your financial institution. Once your financial institution receives settlement, you can credit the customerâs account. Your financial institution is responsible for initiating and tracking its collection requests.
Alternatives to accepting a cheque on collection:
Request an incoming wire â Advise the customer to return the cheque to the sender and request an incoming wire instead. Funds sent by wire may reduce settlement delays and costs. Provide the customer with your financial institutionâs routing instructions. Routing instructions are available as forms for the most common currencies. See the Wires Part for more information. The customer provides the routing instructions to the sender.
Process the cheque on FX Notes Plus (if the currency is accepted). See the FX Notes Plus Part for procedures.
19.2	Initiating a Collection Request
Consider the following when accepting a cheque that is sent on collection:
Conventional rights of recourse donât apply. Financial institutions may encounter significant delays in receiving settlement.
The drawee branchâs handling charges arenât standardized and can be substantial. The cost of processing a collection item may exceed its value. Central 1 doesnât recommend accepting a cheque of $300 (Canadian equivalent) or less if itâs sent on collection.
Financial institutions must send their collection requests directly to the drawee branch. Central 1 doesnât process collection requests.
To initiate a collection request for a cheque that canât be deposited through Central 1:
Verify that the cheque is acceptable for collection. Some cheques canât be accepted even on collection. Central 1 doesnât recommend sending a collection request to countries where obtaining settlement is problematic (e.g., due to prevalent postal fraud). See Chapter 5, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit.
Advise the customer that the settlement period can be lengthy, and that the drawee branch may deduct substantial charges from the settlement amount.
Ensure that the cheque is endorsed by the payee. See Chapter 5, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit, for more information.
Important
: 	The rules for endorsement vary for cheques drawn on foreign financial institutions. The foreign financial institution may accept a âdeposited to the account ofâ stamp, or it may require the payee to endorse the cheque with the payeeâs name exactly as shown on the face of the cheque and with the payeeâs financial institution account number. If the item isnât endorsed as required, the financial institution will return the item.
To avoid possible returns on cheques drawn on foreign financial institutions, including those domiciled in the US, the payee must endorse the cheque with the payeeâs name exactly as shown on the face of the cheque and with the payeeâs financial institution account number.
Important
:	Foreign cheques become stale-dated after six months. Exercise caution when negotiating a cheque that is less than one month before the stale-date. The cheque may become stale-dated by the time it reaches the drawee branch. If this occurs, the cheque is returned to your financial institution.
Stamp the back of the cheque with your financial institutionâs Identification Stamp.
Note:	A cheque that is cleared without an identification stamp can be returned by the financial institution on which itâs drawn and can create risk for the negotiating financial institution. A delay in return caused by the lack of an identification stamp is the responsibility of the negotiating branch of the financial institution.
Stamp the back of the cheque with an âEndorsement Guaranteedâ stamp and record your signature. Usually, there is a line provided for the tellerâs signature.
Ensure that you have a complete mailing address for the drawee branch. If the address isnât provided on the cheque, look it up online.
If there is any doubt about the address of the drawee branch, donât send the collection request. Ask the customer to obtain the address for you or request that the sender provide an incoming wire instead.
Assign a unique reference number to the collection item. The reference number can follow the format: COLL + branch number + year (YY) + sequential number (e.g., COLL62002-16-01).
Record the details of the collection request on an internal register. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use the Outgoing Deposit Collection Items Register, Form 1089, and its Completion Instructions.
Note:	It isnât necessary to use a paper copy of the Outgoing Deposit Collection Items Register, Form 1089. This form is published as an Excel spreadsheet and is fully customizable.
Complete an outgoing deposit collection request. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use the Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, and its Completion Instructions.
Make two copies of the completed Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, or an alternate document for non-subscribers. Keep one copy for your financial institutionâs records. The original and the other copy are for the drawee branch.
Make a copy of the front and back of the cheque and attach it to your financial institutionâs copy of the Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, or an alternate document for non-subscribers. File in a pending collection requests folder.
Send the following documents to the drawee branch:
â
The original plus one copy of the Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, or an alternate document for non-subscribers. The drawee branch may or may not return a copy of the form to your financial institution
â
The original cheque
â
Your financial institutionâs routing instructions. Routing instructions are available as forms for the most common currencies. See the Wires Part for more information.
Monitor the collection item for payment or non-acceptance within the time limit determined by your financial institutionâs policy. You can send a second request to the drawee branch if a response isnât received within a certain time frame.
Related Information
Outgoing Collection Request,
Form 1061
, and Completion Instructions (available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
Outgoing Deposit Collection Items Register,
Form 1089
, and Completion Instructions (available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
19.3	Receiving Settlement or an Unpaid Item Response from the Drawee Branch
Credit the customer when settlement is received from the drawee branch. Settlement is generally provided via incoming wire. See the Wires Part for procedures on receiving incoming wires.
If the drawee branch returns the cheque unpaid, advise the customer. If applicable, debit the customer for any charges associated with the request.
Remove the Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, or the alternate document for non-subscribers, with the attached copies of the cheque from the pending collection requests folder. The drawee branch may or may not return a copy of the Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, or alternate document for non-subscribers, with its response. This form isnât generally sent back to your financial institution since settlement is often via wire transfer.
However, if a copy of the Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, an alternate document for non-subscribers, or a copy of the drawee branchâs own form is provided, attach it to your financial institutionâs copy of the cheque and Outgoing Collection Request, Form 1061, or alternate document for non-subscribers. File the documentation in a completed collection requests folder.
Update the Outgoing Deposit Collection Items Register, Form 1089, or the alternate document for non-subscribers, as required.
Related Information
Outgoing Collection Request,
Form 1061
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
Outgoing Deposit Collection Items Register,
Form 1089
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
END
